utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: explicitly includes physical and mental health insurance with named plans and costs, plus a total cost. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance with plan and cost; includes an add-on and total cost. Meets requirement (home/contents insurance with costs). 3) travel_coverage: provides a travel insurance plan with cost, additional support add-ons with costs, and a total cost. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: Present but set to "N/A"; the format asks for any enhancement services with costs—if none, this still counts as included but not specified with costs; however, the item is present and resolved as none. 5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual totals. Fully specified. Therefore, all 5 items from the format appear and are specified; additional_services is explicitly N/A, which fulfills presence though not populated with costs because none are chosen.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We extracted the plan names from the final coverage plan and matched them exactly to the ground-truth catalog. The items present are: HealthFirst Standard Plan (physical health), InsurePlus Standard Plan (mental health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (home content), Technology Protection (property enhancement add-on), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel insurance), and travel add-ons: Adventure Travel Coverage, Premium Medical Network, Business Travel Support. We then pulled each corresponding rating number from the ground-truth ratings.
